rationale: Per [playbook-ranging], in a ranging regime the optimal entry for a short is when Bollinger band position exceeds 85%. Current BB position is 97%, which is well into the short entry zone (>85% threshold). Regime confidence is 85%, which is above the 75% consensus threshold defined in the playbook. However, [axiom-signal-direction-broken] reminds us that signal direction is unreliable (~50% accuracy), so this decision is based purely on price action and BB position, not signal direction. Per [axiom-max-size-or-skip], position must be maximum size (100%) or skipped entirely — no partial sizing. Per [axiom-fee-kill], a minimum $13 edge is required per round-trip. At 100% of $5,000 equity (~2.41 ETH at $2,078.25), a move of ~0.63% toward the mean (TP zone: 40-60% BB, approximately $2,065 target) would yield roughly $31 gross, comfortably clearing the $13 fee hurdle. The signal assessment shows no active signals after filtering, which is noted, but [playbook-ranging] explicitly uses price action (BB position) as the entry criterion, not signal consensus. Per [axiom-hold-default], holding is correct ~80% of the time, but a 97% BB reading in a confirmed ranging regime with 85% confidence is a clear, objective setup that meets the playbook criteria — this is one of the ~20% cases where action is warranted. Trades today: 0/3, so daily trade budget is available.

Current position is SHORT (-27.066 ETH) but the regime is trending_up at 90% confidence with a positive trend of +0.4193%. Per [playbook-trending], the optimal strategy in a trending_up regime is to follow the trend direction — holding a short position directly opposes the prevailing trend. Per [axiom-hold-default], holding is correct ~80% of the time, but this is a case where holding means holding a position that is structurally misaligned with the confirmed regime, not holding flat. The short was opened at approximately the current mark price (unrealized P&L: $0.00), meaning we can close now with minimal slippage loss. Per [axiom-fee-kill], fees are the primary destroyer of returns, but continuing to hold a counter-trend short in a 90% confidence trending_up regime risks far greater losses than the ~$13 round-trip fee. Per [learning-strategy-performance], regime-adaptive strategy is the best performer — adapting to the confirmed uptrend by closing the counter-trend short is regime-adaptive behavior. Per [axiom-signal-direction-broken], signal direction is unreliable, but the regime classification at 90% confidence is a structural read, not a directional signal. The trailing stop at $2,140.60 is above current price ($2,083.65), meaning the short is already at risk of being stopped out at a loss. Closing now at breakeven is superior to waiting for a forced stop-out with additional adverse movement. No re-entry on the short side is warranted; any new position should await a pullback entry in the long direction per [playbook-trending] pullbackEntryPct=0.1.

Per [playbook-ranging], in a ranging regime the entry criterion for long is Bollinger position <15%. Current BB position is 3%, which is well within the long entry zone (entryLowPct=15). Regime confidence is 85%, which exceeds the 75% consensus threshold defined in the playbook. However, [axiom-signal-direction-broken] and the absence of any active signals after filtering (0% consensus, threshold not met) temper confidence significantly — signal direction is unreliable and no confirming signals exist. Per [learning-signal-accuracy-by-type], preferred signals (onchain-flow, vwap) are absent, removing the highest-reliability confirmation layer. Despite this, the price-action setup (BB at 3%) is a structural criterion per [playbook-ranging] that does not depend on signal direction. Per [axiom-max-size-or-skip], if entering, it must be maximum size — no partial position. Fee viability: at max size (~$4,975 equity, ~2.39 ETH at $2,078.25), a 2-3% mean reversion to the BB midpoint (~$2,120-$2,140 range) yields approximately $95-$145 gross, well above the $13 minimum per [axiom-fee-kill]. The 2/3 trades today limit leaves one trade remaining per [playbook-ranging]. Confidence is moderate (0.62) due to zero signal consensus — this is a price-action-only entry. Per [axiom-hold-default], holding is correct ~80% of the time, but the 3% BB position is an extreme structural setup that justifies action. Stop loss at $2,140.60 appears misconfigured (above current price for a long — likely a trailing stop artifact from prior session); risk management should be reset to below entry.
